query:
here is the recommendation on 476.  thoughts? DIAGNOSIS + DESIGN REVIEW COMPLETE — Awaiting approval.
                                                                                                                  
  Root Cause: The promotion SELECT in co_access_promotion_tick.rs (~line 215) has no status filter. Compaction
  (step 2 in background.rs) correctly deletes GRAPH_EDGES where either endpoint is quarantined (status = 3).      
  Promotion (step 3) then re-reads the same co_access rows with no JOIN to entries, re-inserts both directions for
   the 32 quarantined-endpoint pairs, and the cycle repeats every tick.                                           
                                                                                                                
  Affected Files:                                                                                                 
  - crates/unimatrix-store/src/co_access_promotion_tick.rs — primary fix
  - crates/unimatrix-store/tests/co_access_promotion_tick_tests.rs — new tests                                    
                                                                                                                
  Proposed Fix (investigator's approach + architect amendments):                                                  
  1. Add JOIN entries ea ON ea.id = ca.entry_id_a AND ea.status != ?3 and matching JOIN entries eb ON ... to both 
  the outer SELECT and the scalar max_count subquery.                                                             
  2. Add .bind(Status::Quarantined as u8 as i64) as a third bind parameter (currently only 2 binds exist — missing
   this would cause silent wrong results via SQL NULL).                                                           
  3. Three new tests in co_access_promotion_tick_tests.rs: (a) one quarantined endpoint → 0 edges promoted, (b)   
  both quarantined → 0 edges promoted, (c) mixed batch → only active-both-endpoints pairs promoted. Tests must 
  seed real entries rows with quarantined status, not rely on non-existent IDs as a proxy.                        
  4. Write-time filter: withdrawn from this PR. The oscillation fix is tick-side only. A comment will be added at
  the analytics write site documenting that quarantine filtering is intentionally deferred to the tick-side JOIN. 
  Stale-pair accumulation filed as a follow-up.                                                                   
                                               
  Design Review: APPROVED WITH NOTES — no blocking architectural concerns. The JOIN approach matches the existing 
  compaction pattern and runs on a background tick (no hot-path risk). Two amendments (subquery filter + third    
  bind) are correctness-critical and addressed in the approach above.
                                                                                                                  
  Risk Assessment: Low. SQL change is narrow and bounded. Worst case of a subtle bug: promotion promotes nothing  
  (safe — graph just doesn't grow) or promotes everything (reverts to current state). Third bind omission is the
  highest-risk pitfall and is explicitly covered by the new tests.                                                
                                                                                                                
  Missing Test: Tests for quarantined-endpoint pairs in co_access_promotion_tick_tests.rs (zero exist today).

entries.title: co_access promotion SELECT missing quarantine-status filter causes compaction/promotion oscillation
Co-access promotion tick: quarantine JOIN required on both endpoints and in max_count subquery
Promotion tick SELECT must JOIN entries for quarantine filter on both outer query and normalization subquery
